dom_scan.interpretation: SPA hit /rest/thread backend 403 -> rendered private/login overlay; no APF warning bubble injected

comparison_vs_703.703_v7c: visibility=public sent in SSE block payload but failed to bypass backend ACL on thread fetch -> private modal shown
comparison_vs_703.706_v7d: engine designed to intercept /rest/thread/<UUID> follow-up GET on same connection within 5min of PII block; did NOT trigger -- backend served 403 just like 703
comparison_vs_703.new_symptom: ERR_HTTP2_PROTOCOL_ERROR appeared on thread + analytics + homepage-widgets requests -- suggests engine code path is partially active but emitting invalid HTTP/2 framing rather than a clean synthesized 200 response. SPA retries, gets 403 from backend on retry.

recommendation: V7-D intercept code path is active (evidence: ERR_HTTP2_PROTOCOL_ERROR on first attempt) but NOT producing a valid HTTP/2 response. Likely root causes: (1) HEADERS frame missing or malformed for synthesized response, (2) DATA frame size/length mismatch, (3) END_STREAM flag not set, (4) connection-level frame ordering. Browser cancels malformed stream and SPA retries -> backend ACL returns 403. Next: capture engine-side packet dump or HTTP/2 frame log for the 7d1f2f75 thread fetch and verify HEADERS+DATA+END_STREAM are emitted in spec-compliant order.
next_action: engine code review of on_http2_request synthesis path; ensure response shape uses Content-Type: application/json and END_STREAM flag set on final DATA frame
